equivalence_argument: The builder and verifier call shared pricing and propagate ordinary errors, but the LLM pricing function still converts executor-reported error outputs into a constant fee, so exact no-fallback settlement pricing is not fully preserved.

checked_queries: LLM executor returned an error response; using constant error fee
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Async settlement creation/verification does not fail closed for LLM executor outputs with has_error=true; those outputs receive a constant executor fee instead of exact LLM pricing failure.
related_but_insufficient_code: prepare_fulfilled_async_settlement delegates to calculate_async_fees and would skip settlement on Err, and verify_settlements rejects fee-calculation Err or component mismatches.

compute_llm_executor_fee returns Err for missing output, malformed output, and missing models, but catches decode_llm_response errors prefixed with LLM_HAS_ERROR_PREFIX and returns Ok(LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI).
The local test llm_has_error_returns_constant_fee asserts the constant-fee behavior for executor-reported LLM errors.

notes: Confirmed gap in the shared async-fees path used by settlement building and verification.
rationale: The primary diff removes a unified fallback-style fee path and replaces it with explicit LLM compute_llm_executor_fee handling that maps errors to transaction skipping, while HTTP operations continue to use input/output byte pricing. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When creating async settlement transactions, LLM async executions must compute executor fees from the actual execution result using exact LLM pricing and fail settlement creation if that pricing fails; non-LLM HTTP executions use byte-based executor pricing plus commitment and inclusion fees.
suggested_falsification_tests: Attempt to create settlement for an LLM async result with malformed or missing pricing data and assert settlement creation errors instead of falling back; for a valid LLM result assert exact fee plus commitment and inclusion fees, and for an HTTP result assert byte-based executor fee plus the same common components.
summary: LLM settlement pricing still accepts executor-reported error outputs as a constant fee.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Build a fulfilled LLM settlement whose executor output sets has_error=true and assert the invariant expects settlement creation to fail or verification to reject; current compute_llm_executor_fee returns Ok(LLM_ERROR_EXECUTOR_FEE_WEI).
title: Async settlement fees use exact LLM pricing without fallback
